gaps.description: FIX-4 WIRED branch has no properly-typed code-authoring subtask, and the wire (if it lands) falls outside the dependency sets of subtask 8 (code validation) and subtask 9 (live verify FIX-4). Main plan routes FIX-4 wiring into subtask 4 (Agent: researcher) and 'records' it in subtask 7 (Agent: implementer, but explicitly doc-only, not a code change). Subtask-4.md adds a contradictory third option ('OR the orchestrator inserts an implementer follow-up'). subtask 8 (Depends On 2,3,7) validates a wire 'recorded in subtask 7' that carries no code diff; subtask 9 (Depends On 7) would live-verify a wire whose code-landing subtask is not in its dependency chain.
gaps.evidence: [verified: docs/v2-design/plan-v2-ux-fidelity-fixes-subtask-4.md (Agent: researcher / 'OR the orchestrator inserts an implementer follow-up', l.11)] vs [verified: docs/v2-design/plan-v2-ux-fidelity-fixes-subtask-7.md ('documentation deliverable, not a code change', l.9)] vs plan l.64 (subtask 9 Depends On: 7) and l.84 ('any FIX-4 wire from subtask 4 recorded in subtask 7')
gaps.fix_hint: [dimension: D-plan-dag-cycle] Add an explicit implementer-typed subtask for the FIX-4 WIRED branch (or scope subtask 7 to include the wiring code), make subtask 8 and subtask 9 depend on that code-landing subtask, and remove the researcher-lands-code provision from subtask-4.md so the WIRED branch has a single unambiguous owner. should-be-issue
